---
title: "QLoRA with torchao"
description: "Quantize the base model with torchao instead of bitsandbytes for compile-friendly, FSDP2-native LoRA/QLoRA."
format:
  html:
    toc: true
---

## Background

`bitsandbytes` is the historical backend for QLoRA in Axolotl. It works well in
single-GPU and FSDP1 setups, but its 4-bit kernels graph-break under
`torch.compile` and its `Params4bit` storage needs custom patches to cooperate
with FSDP2.

[torchao](https://github.com/pytorch/ao) provides tensor-subclass quantization
(`AffineQuantizedTensor`, `NF4Tensor`) that:

- compiles cleanly with `torch.compile` (no graph breaks for INT4/INT8
  weight-only quant);
- ships native FSDP2 support via DTensor;
- shares its codebase with QAT / PTQ.

Axolotl exposes both backends via the structured `model_quantization_config`
field, a flat union discriminated on its `backend` key.

## Usage

`model_quantization_config` is a discriminated union: `backend` is one of
`bnb` / `torchao` / `mxfp4` / `fp8`. QLoRA is not a separate adapter type —
it is `adapter: lora` plus a 4-bit base quant. The bnb / torchao backends
require `adapter: lora`; without an adapter the config is rejected.

```yaml
# torchao QLoRA
adapter: lora
model_quantization_config:
  backend: torchao
  weight_dtype: int4     # one of: int4, nf4, nvfp4, int8, fp8
  # group_size: 128      # defaults: int4/int8 → 128, nf4 → 64, nvfp4 → 16

# int4 only: required for training (see note below)
lora_mlp_kernel: true
lora_qkv_kernel: true
lora_o_kernel: true
```

```yaml
# bnb QLoRA (replaces `adapter: qlora` + `load_in_4bit: true`)
adapter: lora
model_quantization_config:
  backend: bnb
  weight_dtype: nf4      # mirrored into load_in_4bit for the loader
```

```yaml
# bnb 8-bit LoRA (replaces `load_in_8bit: true`)
adapter: lora
model_quantization_config:
  backend: bnb
  weight_dtype: int8     # mirrored into load_in_8bit for the loader
```

| `backend` | `weight_dtype`     | Training style    | Quant config installed       |
|-----------|--------------------|-------------------|------------------------------|
| `bnb`     | `nf4`              | QLoRA (4-bit)     | `BitsAndBytesConfig(load_in_4bit=True)` |
| `bnb`     | `int8`             | 8-bit LoRA        | `BitsAndBytesConfig(load_in_8bit=True)` |
| `torchao` | `int4`             | QLoRA (4-bit)     | `Int4WeightOnlyConfig` (tile-packed tinygemm layout, CUDA cc >= 8.0) |
| `torchao` | `nf4`              | QLoRA (4-bit)     | `NF4WeightOnlyConfig`        |
| `torchao` | `nvfp4`            | QLoRA (4-bit)     | `NVFP4WeightOnlyConfig` (torch ≥ 2.8; `group_size` must be 16 or unset) |
| `torchao` | `int8`             | weight-only LoRA  | `Int8WeightOnlyConfig`       |
| `torchao` | `fp8`              | weight-only LoRA  | `Float8WeightOnlyConfig`     |

::: {.callout-important}
`weight_dtype: int4` requires `lora_mlp_kernel`, `lora_qkv_kernel`, and
`lora_o_kernel` for training. torchao's int4 tensors implement linear as a
bare quantized matmul with no autograd support, so input gradients can only
flow through axolotl's LoRA kernels, which dequantize explicitly in their own
forward/backward. `nf4` / `nvfp4` / `fp8` dequantize (or ship a backward) in
torchao itself and train without the kernels; the validator enforces this for
int4.
:::

### Deprecations

There is only one canonical shape for base-quant LoRA training:

```yaml
adapter: lora
model_quantization_config:
  backend: bnb                  # or torchao / mxfp4 / fp8
  weight_dtype: nf4
```

The following legacy shapes keep working — they are translated to the
canonical form at config load and emit a `DEPRECATED:` warning. They will
be removed in a future release:

- `adapter: qlora` (with or without `load_in_4bit: true`) → `adapter: lora`
  + `model_quantization_config: {backend: bnb, weight_dtype: nf4}`. QLoRA is
  just "LoRA with a 4-bit base quant"; there is no separate adapter type.
- `load_in_4bit: true` (alone) → `model_quantization_config:
  {backend: bnb, weight_dtype: nf4}`.
- `load_in_8bit: true` (alone) → `model_quantization_config:
  {backend: bnb, weight_dtype: int8}`.
- `model_quantization_config: Mxfp4Config` (string) with
  `model_quantization_config_kwargs: …` — keeps working as-is; the warning
  points at `model_quantization_config: {backend: mxfp4, config_kwargs: …}`.
- `model_quantization_config: FineGrainedFP8Config` (string) — same, with
  `model_quantization_config: {backend: fp8, config_kwargs: …}`.

## Mxfp4Config / FineGrainedFP8Config

Pre-quantized checkpoints typically encode their quant scheme in the
checkpoint's own `quantization_config`; you don't need to set
`model_quantization_config` in that case. For the cases where you do want to
override at load time, the legacy string form keeps working:

```yaml
# Legacy string form (still works)
model_quantization_config: Mxfp4Config
model_quantization_config_kwargs: {}
```

…and has a parallel structured form:

```yaml
model_quantization_config:
  backend: mxfp4
  config_kwargs: {}
```

```yaml
model_quantization_config:
  backend: fp8
  config_kwargs: {}
```

## MXFP4 (MoE only)

MXFP4 has no weight-only torchao config for arbitrary linear layers, so
`weight_dtype: mxfp4` under `backend: torchao` is rejected at schema
validation. For Mixture-of-Experts models, the MXFP4 LoRA path lives behind
`quantize_moe_experts: true` with `lora_target_parameters` matching the fused
3D expert tensors (`gate_up_proj` / `down_proj`). See
[Expert Quantization](expert_quantization.qmd) for that flow.

## Mixed-quant models

`backend: torchao` is a single-knob shorthand that installs *one*
`TorchAoConfig` covering every linear layer in the model. It does *not*
compose with other quantization mechanisms — combining them would produce
silent overrides or fights over the same tensors. The validator and loader
reject the conflicts explicitly:

| Combination                                                       | Outcome |
|-------------------------------------------------------------------|---------|
| `backend: torchao` + `quantize_moe_experts: true`                 | rejected at validation |
| `backend: torchao` + `gptq: true`                                 | rejected at validation |
| `backend: torchao` + `qat` / `ptq` / `fp8` training               | rejected at validation |
| `backend: torchao` + contradicting `load_in_4bit` / `load_in_8bit`| rejected at validation |
| `backend: torchao` + checkpoint with embedded quant               | rejected at load time |

Modern quantized checkpoints encode the mix via a per-module **exclusion
list** in their own `quantization_config`. For example,
[`amd/Kimi-K2.6-MXFP4`](https://huggingface.co/amd/Kimi-K2.6-MXFP4) ships
with `quant_method: quark`, MXFP4 weights, and ~305 excluded modules
(every attention projection, `lm_head`, the vision tower, the
mm_projector). Loading that checkpoint just lets transformers honor the
checkpoint's own scheme — attention layers stay in their native dtype,
only the listed modules get quantized.

If you want **experts in MXFP4 + attention in bf16**, drop
`model_quantization_config` entirely and pick one of:

- Load a checkpoint that already carries the right `quantization_config`
  (gpt-oss MXFP4, AMD Quark MXFP4, AWQ/GPTQ, …). Axolotl forwards the
  checkpoint's config to transformers unchanged, exclusion list and all.
- For a bf16 MoE checkpoint where you want MXFP4 experts at load time,
  set `quantize_moe_experts: true`. Attention stays in its checkpoint
  dtype; the expert tensors get quantized via the path landed in #3663.

Attention-only LoRA on a MoE model with quantized experts is the natural
training-time recipe; see [Expert Quantization](expert_quantization.qmd).

## Constraints

- **DoRA** works with torchao `int4` / `int8` / `fp8` / `nvfp4`, but **not**
  `nf4`: PEFT's `dora_init` calls `weight.dequantize()`, and
  `NF4Tensor.dequantize` is a 2-arg staticmethod, so init raises a
  `TypeError`. Other PEFT extras follow standard LoRA rules.
- **Merging adapters** with `axolotl merge-lora` requires
  `merge_method: legacy`. The memory-efficient merger simulates bnb's NF4
  quantization and does not yet understand torchao tensor subclasses.
- **PEFT's `TorchaoLoraLinear`** only supports INT8. Axolotl patches
  `dispatch_torchao` so INT4 / NF4 weights fall back to standard `Linear` LoRA
  layers (the kernels dequantize the base weight on access).
- `load_in_4bit` and `load_in_8bit` are bnb-only. They cannot be combined with
  `backend: torchao` — the validator rejects the config. (The structured `bnb`
  form mirrors itself into these flags for the loader; don't set them by hand.)

## Example config

See [`examples/llama-3/qlora-torchao.yaml`](https://github.com/axolotl-ai-cloud/axolotl/blob/main/examples/llama-3/qlora-torchao.yaml).

## FSDP2

torchao tensor subclasses are first-class DTensor citizens, so the bnb-specific
FSDP2 patches (sharded-init shims, `Linear8bitLt` save patch) are skipped
automatically when the torchao backend is in use.

```yaml
adapter: lora
model_quantization_config:
  backend: torchao
  weight_dtype: nf4

fsdp_version: 2
fsdp_config:
  fsdp_offload_params: false
  fsdp_cpu_ram_efficient_loading: true
  fsdp_sharding_strategy: FULL_SHARD
  fsdp_state_dict_type: SHARDED_STATE_DICT
  fsdp_auto_wrap_policy: TRANSFORMER_BASED_WRAP
```
